title: Erodium absinthoides cinereum
taxonomy: Erodium absinthoides cinereum is a subspecies of the plant Erodium absinthoides. It belongs to the family Geraniaceae, commonly known as the geranium family. This family is a group of flowering plants native to Europe, Asia, and Africa. The genus Erodium is further divided into several species, including Erodium absinthoides cinereum, which is a narrow-leaved variant of the species. The classification of Erodium absinthoides cinereum reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.

intro: Erodium absinthoides cinereum, commonly known as cinereous heron's bill or cinereous storksbill, is a species of flowering plant in the family Geraniaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and inflammation, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Erodium absinthoides cinereum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat gastrointestinal disorders, reduce inflammation, and support immune function.
